body.title: /review writes review_findings records; reads them on next run to self-improve
body.result: Created review_findings table + review_manager.py for writing verdicts to DB. /review now logs findings with confidence tracking for self-learning calibration
body.description:
Every /review run writes a record: what it flagged, whether user overrode, whether sprint-time later confirmed/refuted. Next /review reads recent records and adjusts confidence on recurring patterns.

## Storage
Infra table `review_findings`:
- id, created_at, ticket_id (may be null for standalone reviews)
- flag_type (duplicate, already-done, blocked-by, size-mismatch, scope-creep, high-inertia, missing-test-plan)
- flag_detail (json)
- user_response (accepted | overrode | ignored)
- sprint_outcome (confirmed | refuted | n/a) — backfilled when sprint closes

## Reader side
On each /review, compute recent hit-rate per flag_type per subsystem area. Raise confidence threshold for flags with low hit-rate; lower for high hit-rate.

## Blocked by
- T-review-as-filing-time-skill (needs /review to exist in its new shape)

## Not in scope
- Building /review itself.
